name: Current Affairs in Indian Polity (9): The Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026

sourceRefs.label: prsindia.org/scconline.com - Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 (introduced 23 March 2026, referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee)

researchNotes: Tenth Polity current-affairs topic (9th lap). A useful companion to the Day-87 Jan Vishwas Bill topic — another major 2026 decriminalisation-focused bill, this time targeting corporate/company law specifically, and currently under JPC scrutiny rather than already passed.

reading.intro: Covers the Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 — amending the Companies Act, 2013 and the LLP Act, 2008.

qa.question: When was the Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 introduced, and what is its current status?
qa.answer: Introduced in the Lok Sabha on 23 March 2026; referred to a Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) for comprehensive clause-by-clause scrutiny and stakeholder consultation.

qa.question: Which 2 Acts does this Bill amend?
qa.answer: The Companies Act, 2013 and the Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) Act, 2008.

qa.question: What is the Bill's core decriminalisation approach?
qa.answer: It replaces imprisonment/fine for several offences (e.g., failure to furnish information to the Registrar, violations of book-of-accounts requirements) with CIVIL PENALTIES instead.

qa.question: What change does the Bill make to Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) compliance?
qa.answer: It raises/adjusts the net profit threshold for CSR applicability to ₹10 crore (or another prescribed sum) — companies meeting prescribed conditions below this threshold won't need to comply with CSR provisions.

qa.question: What new flexibility does the Bill provide for Annual General Meetings (AGMs)?
qa.answer: Companies may hold AGMs physically, via video conferencing, or other audio-visual means — but must still hold a PHYSICAL meeting at least once every 3 years.

qa.question: Which regulatory body does the Bill designate as the new 'Valuation Authority'?
qa.answer: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) — responsible for registering/recognising valuers and recommending valuation standards to the government.

questions.prompt: The Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 was introduced in the Lok Sabha on:
The Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 amends the Companies Act, 2013 and which other Act?
The Bill's decriminalisation approach replaces imprisonment/fine for several offences with:
Under the Bill, companies must hold a physical AGM at least once every:
The Bill designates which body as the new 'Valuation Authority'?
questions.explanation: IBBI (Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India).
